County Superintendent OF
Schools Watkins has nearly COM
pleted his annual report on the
schools Of Spokane county. There
are 1853 districts this year as
against 1877 last year The total
receipts are $621,076 as against
$664,294 last year, the difference be-
ing for the most part made up of
receipts from the sale Of bonds last
year. Teachers this year received
$316.0S4 as against $219.360 last
year, the difference being chiefly
caused by higher salaries demanded
in year wnen teachers were
scarce.


The total expenditures are this
year $539.1S9 as against $537, 0S5.
Within the year the assessed valua-
tlon of property within the dis
tricts has increased from $4,000.
GOA to $36.356,150. In the same
time the average salary of male
teachers has advNced from $3.50
per month to SIS, while salaries
OF female teachers have gone up
from $6S.64 to $69.14.
